channel.title: Home
channel.description: Developer, tech lead, recovering designer. 20 years shipping things.

item.title: Building My First Public Skill for Claude Code: The 1on1
item.description: I built a /1on1 slash command for Claude Code that runs a structured professional check-in between me and my AI collaborator. This is the story of how and why.

item.title: sudo on Linux with Touch ID (and live to tell the tale)
item.description: Configure 1Password's SSH agent to use Touch ID on your remote Linux server.

item.title: Paid Services Offered for Free Due to COVID-19
item.description: Resources to help get through lockdown.

item.title: Ten Things I Learned After Three Years at the Gym
Hello, World!
item.description: This post is ten years late.

item.title: The Web Storms the Desktop: Silverlight
item.description: Microsoft Silverlight — the Adobe AIR rival built on WPF and XAML — reviewed from the perspective of a web developer accustomed to open technologies.

item.title: The Web Storms the Desktop: Mozilla Prism
item.description: Mozilla Prism lets anyone turn their favourite web services into desktop applications — remarkably simply, and without writing a single line of code.

item.title: The Web Storms the Desktop: Adobe AIR
item.description: Adobe AIR lets web developers build cross-platform desktop applications using the same technologies they already know: HTML, JavaScript, and Flash.

item.title: Opera Mobile 9.5. Presto!
item.description: Opera announces Opera Mobile 9.5, featuring a ground-up rewritten Presto engine, the Futhark JavaScript engine, and Flash support — unveiled at Mobile World Congress 2008.

item.title: Microhoo?
item.description: Microsoft launches a $44 billion bid to acquire Yahoo! — a deal that could become the largest purchase in the history of the internet.

item.title: On Facebook + Nokia
item.description: Rumours of a Facebook–Nokia alliance echo the old browser wars and raise the question of whether the social web is about to make the same mistakes all over again.

item.title: Workaholics
item.description: A reflection on the difference between fear-driven workaholism and genuine passion for work, sparked by a Seth Godin essay.

item.title: Hype, Hype, Baby!
item.description: An analysis of Apple's marketing around its major events: from carefully controlled rumours to the viral effect that turns every launch into an internet phenomenon.

item.title: IE8 ♥ Acid2: Better Late Than Never
item.description: IE8 announces it passes the Acid2 test — a historic milestone for web developers who have spent years battling Internet Explorer's compatibility issues.

item.title: Android, iPhone. The Web Reaches Mobile
item.description: Google and Apple are leading a mobile web revolution with Android and iPhone — two platforms that promise to change forever how we access the internet from our phones.
